content:
I noticed that many commits have Message-ID tags instead of Link tags. Is the dim tooling adding those tags for you? When I use it, it still uses Link tags, and when I sent the drm-rust-next PR, the tool gave me a bunch of errors that I had to work around by modifying the script:

Using drm/drm-next as the upstream

dim: ERROR: issues in commits detected, aborting

does your version contain https://gitlab.freedesktop.org/drm/maintainer-tools/-/commit/f867e605613af1770f90c4b0afd4a8f06424d1f0 ?
looking at that change it possibly depends on changes only in b4's master branch
Yes, I did notice that the script started using patch.msgid.link instead of lore, but it's still a Link tag.
ah, I misread. that could be an intermittent change. It took a while until Linus was ok with Link tags with patch.msgid.link instead of lore.kernel.org as domain
In my case Message-ID tags are added by b4. I stopped using Link: (and forced dim to push my patches despite their absence) after Linus complained about it, but has the situation changed?

It looks like this is the conclusion that dim follows:
https://lore.kernel.org/tools/CAMuHMdVX58j6kfGBdv0gBnOSUvf5fVoe1q1=zva1fMo-4undqg@mail.gmail.com/T/#m1647bc9d305934f0b88132a5f7dbd859709e5b92

aha, I completely missed the memo. Alright, I will use Link instead of Message-ID, sorry for the inconsistency for this release.

I kinda liked Message-ID as it is independent of any link service, and clearly indicates this is about this patch and not some context, but apparently that's the role https://patch.msgid.link will fulfill as well so all is good (as long as that domain remains active, that is ^_^;)
Has b4 been updated to use patch.msgid.link? Last time I used it, it still used the lore.kernel.org link
I think the latest version does do it, but for users of dim it's handled for you in dim
Hmm.. still using lore link in b4 0.14.3 for me
Yeah, I just figured it out myself as well ;-) need to use upstream b4 for that atm I think

Little bit late, but maybe it's still useful (maybe for other cases). You can also add things like the following to your .gitconfig:

[b4]
        linkmask = https://patch.msgid.link/%s
I thought I was on a version with linkmask default already updated, but then it turns out that the linkmask change hasn't landed in a tagged release of b4 yet

Danilo Krummrich said:

Little bit late, but maybe it's still useful (maybe for other cases). You can also add things like the following to your .gitconfig:

[b4]
        linkmask = https://patch.msgid.link/%s

Emoji: Life changer :)
